title: Web Development Fundamentals
description: Test your knowledge of core web development concepts including HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and modern frameworks.

questions.question: Which HTML element is used to define the structure of an HTML document?
questions.explanation: The <html> element is the root element that wraps all content in an HTML document. <!DOCTYPE html> is a declaration, not an element.

questions.question: What does the CSS property 'box-sizing: border-box' do?
options: Adds a border around every element
Includes padding and border in the element's total width and height
Removes all margins from the element
Sets the element's display to block
questions.explanation: With border-box, the width and height properties include the content, padding, and border — but not the margin. This makes layout calculations much more predictable.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a valid JavaScript data type?
questions.explanation: JavaScript does not have a separate Float type. Numbers are represented by the Number type (IEEE 754 double-precision floating point) or BigInt for arbitrary-precision integers.

questions.question: What does the acronym 'REST' stand for in the context of web APIs?
questions.explanation: REST (Representational State Transfer) is an architectural style for designing networked applications, introduced by Roy Fielding in his 2000 doctoral dissertation.

questions.question: In React, what hook is used to perform side effects in function components?
questions.explanation: useEffect lets you synchronize a component with an external system — it runs after render and can handle subscriptions, data fetching, and DOM manipulation.

questions.question: What HTTP status code indicates a resource was successfully created?
questions.explanation: 201 Created is returned when a request has been fulfilled and a new resource has been created. It is typically used in response to POST requests.

questions.question: What command initializes a new Git repository in the current directory?
questions.explanation: Running 'git init' creates a new .git subdirectory in the current folder, setting up all the files Git needs to begin tracking changes.

questions.question: Which CSS layout system is best suited for two-dimensional layouts (rows and columns)?
questions.explanation: CSS Grid is designed for two-dimensional layouts, allowing you to control both rows and columns simultaneously. Flexbox is better suited for one-dimensional layouts.

questions.question: What does the 'async' keyword do when placed before a function declaration?
options: Makes the function run in a separate thread
Makes the function return a Promise
Pauses the function until all other code finishes
Prevents the function from throwing errors
questions.explanation: An async function always returns a Promise. If the function returns a value, the Promise resolves with that value. This enables the use of 'await' inside the function body.

questions.question: What is the default port number used by a Next.js development server?
questions.explanation: Next.js runs its development server on port 3000 by default. You can change this with the -p flag, e.g., 'next dev -p 4000'.
